original_abstract:
In this study, we explored the chemical reactivity of 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (chalcone 3) toward various active methylene derivatives, utilizing pressurized microwave irradiation as an efficient and sustainable "green" energy source. Under controlled pressure at 70 °C, chalcone 3 was reacted with ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone, and thioglycolic acid to yield a series of heterocyclic scaffolds, specifically 2-hydroxyphenylcyanopyridone, 2-hydroxyphenyl acetylcyclohexanone, and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one derivatives. Additionally, the oxidative cyclization of chalcone 3 with hydrogen peroxide afforded a corresponding chromen-4-one derivative. The structures of all newly synthesized compounds were rigorously characterized using FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, and mass spectrometry.

Biological evaluation revealed that these heterocycles possess potent antioxidant properties, with several compounds demonstrating radical scavenging activities comparable to vitamin C. Notably, the presence of hydroxyl groups significantly enhanced their inhibitory effects. To further elucidate these findings, molecular docking simulations were performed on compound 12 against two protein targets (PDB ID: 1DH2 and 3RP8). The results showed that compound 12 exhibits superior binding affinity and shorter bond lengths compared to ascorbic acid. Furthermore, density functional theory (DFT) calculations at the B3LYP/6-31G (d,p) level were used to optimize the structures and identify key physical descriptors. The structure of compound 12 was also confirmed via single-crystal X-ray diffraction and Hirshfeld surface analysis to evaluate intermolecular electrostatic interactions. Theoretical results showed an excellent correlation with experimental bond lengths, angles, FT-IR, and NMR data, validating the robustness of our computational model.
humanized_abstract:
The chemical reactivity of 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (chalcone 3) toward active methylene compounds was investigated using pressurized microwave-assisted method in view of its sustainable energy feature. Thus, reaction of chalcone 3 with ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone and thioglycolic acid at 70 °C and under controlled pressure afforded novel heterocyclic compounds namely 2-hydroxyphenylcyanopyridone, 2-hydroxyphenyl acetylcyclohexanone and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one derivatives. The oxidative cyclization of the chalcone 3 with hydrogen peroxide afforded the corresponding chromen-4-one derivative. All the newly synthesized compounds were characterized by FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR and mass spectra.

In vitro assessment of the isolated heterocyclic compounds revealed that they exhibited strong antioxidant activity while some compounds showed strong scavenging activity comparable to vitamin C. The presence of hydroxyl (-OH) group(s) in the molecules was found to be responsible for the high inhibitory activity of the isolated compounds. Moreover, molecular docking studies of compound 12 with protein targets (PDB ID: 1DH2 and 3RP8) revealed that compound 12 showed more affinity compared to ascorbic acid and shorter bond length. Optimization of the structure of compound 12 was achieved by means of density functional theory (DFT) calculations at the B3LYP/6-31G (d,p) level. The structure of compound 12 was further confirmed via single-crystal X-ray diffraction (SCXRD) analysis and Hirshfeld surface analysis that helps to understand the intermolecular electrostatic interactions. The experimental bond lengths and angles as well as FT-IR and 1 H NMR data were found to be in good agreement with the predicted values and hence validating the accuracy of the computational model.

document.output:
